item.title: Christopher Nolan’s Closet Picks
item.author: CRITERION
item.content:encoded:
Subscribe to Criterion: https://bit.ly/4vlR0aj
The filmmaker shares how THE TESTAMENT OF DR. MABUSE influenced THE DARK KNIGHT, talks about the importance of Martin Scorsese’s World Cinema Project, and praises the way David Lynch evokes dream logic on-screen.
Christopher’s Picks: THE KILLERS (Don Siegel, 1964) JOHN CASSAVETES: FIVE FILMS FOR ALL MAKIND (Al Reinert, 1989) THE COMPLETE MR. ARKADIN (Orson Welles, 1955) THE TESTAMENT OF DR. MABUSE (Fritz Lang, 1933) THE APU TRILOGY (Satyajit Ray) MARTIN SCORSESE’S WORLD CINEMA PROJECT NO. 3 MALCOLM X (Spike Lee, 1992) LOST HIGHWAY (David Lynch, 1997) THE QATSI TRILOGY (Godfrey Reggio) THE PLAYER (Robert Altman, 1992) BOYHOOD (Richard Linklater, 2014) ONE FALSE MOVE (Carl Franklin, 1992) THE BLACK STALLION (Carroll Ballard, 1979) CITIZEN KANE (Orson Welles, 1941)
All are available from criterion.com (and at 50% off from Barnes & Noble through July 26): https://www.criterion.com/shop/collection/984-christopher-nolan-s-closet-picks
And JOHN CASSAVETES: FIVE FILMS, FOR ALL MANKIND, THE COMPLETE MR. ARKADIN, THE TESTAMENT OF DR. MABUSE, THE APU TRILOGY, LOST HIGHWAY, THE PLAYER, BOYHOOD, and all the films in MARTIN SCORSESE’S WORLD CINEMA PROJECT are now currently playing on our streaming service, The Criterion Channel: https://www.criterionchannel.com

item.title: The Most Disgusting Sci Fi Film I've Ever Seen
item.author: The Kino Corner
item.content:encoded:

I also run a tech channel for all the tech savvy people who might watch this video. The channel is @FUTOTECH
Also, if you want to read my script "The Radical," it's available on my Patreon.

Movies shown Hard to Be a God On the Silver Globe War of the Worlds Starman 2001 A Space Odyssey Stalker Solaris Close Encounters of the Third Kind Jason and the Argonauts The Day the Earth Stood Still Walking the Streets of Moscow Ivan's Childhood
Music Blue Danube by Strauss Mahler - Symphony No.4 in G major - 3. Ruhevoil Cello Sonata in G Minor, Op. 65 - III. Largo Jesse Gallagher - Maestro Tlakaelel Esther Abrami - No.4 Piano Journey Tchaikovsky - 1812 Overture (by Tchaikosvky) String Quartet no. 2 - II. Andante molto sostenuto Peer Gynt Suite no. 1, Op. 46 - II. Aase's Death

item.title: Mark Kermode reviews Ask E. Jean
item.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
item.content:encoded:
#MarkKermode #SimonMayo #FilmReviews
E. Jean Carroll won two lawsuits against Donald Trump for defamation and battery. Beyond that, she built a career as a successful magazine writer and authored one of America's longest-running advice columns.
To hear more, follow Kermode and Mayo's Take wherever you get your podcasts: https://podfollow.com/1616559297

item.title: Mark Kermode reviews Pinocchio: Unstrung
item.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
item.content:encoded:

A dark coming-of-age reimagining of Pinocchio, this unsettling tale follows the iconic puppet's disturbing journey toward becoming real.
To hear more, follow Kermode and Mayo's Take wherever you get your podcasts: https://podfollow.com/1616559297

item.title: Mark Kermode reviews Animal Farm
item.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
item.content:encoded:
#MarkKermode #SimonMayo #FilmReviews
A satirical fable following an animal revolution as pigs seize power and the farm descends into a ruthless dictatorship-fulfilling Orwell's warning about the dangers of communism.
To hear more, follow Kermode and Mayo's Take wherever you get your podcasts: https://podfollow.com/1616559297

item.title: Are some ANIMAL FARM adaptations more equal than others? With ANDY SERKIS
item.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
item.content:encoded:
The Take is now on Patreon: www.patreon.com/kermodeandmayo Become a Vanguardista or an Ultra Vanguardista to get video episodes of Take Two every week, plus member-only chat rooms, polls and submissions to influence the show, behind-the-scenes photos and videos, the monthly Redactor’s Roundup newsletter, and access to a new fortnightly LIVE show - a raucous, unfiltered lunchtime special with the Good Doctors, new features, and live chat so you can heckle, vote, and have your questions read out in real time.
We have a very ‘precious’ guest for you this week… yes, it’s Andy Serkis, AKA Gollum himself. The actor, director, and motion capturer extraordinaire chats to Simon about his new film Animal Farm - an animated adaptation of Orwell’s classic story for a young audience. With Seth Rogan putting a comic spin on Orwell’s porky totalitarian Napoleon, and a key new character and third act added, Serkis’ child-friendly version has attracted some ire already - but is it fair? Listen in to find out where Mark stands.
Plus, we’ve got two more reviews of what’s out this week for anyone not going to see The Odyssey. First up, Pinocchio Unstrung - the latest in Twisted Childhood’s series of slasher-fied, copyright free children’s classics. Then there’s Ask E. Jean, a powerful doc about American columnist E. Jean Carroll, who took on Trump in the courts and won. Plus, the Laughter Lift as usual, and we’re also debuting a technophobic new feature ‘AI don’t like that’ - a home for the ever-increasing correspondence about the terrifying ascendency of our future AI overlords.
You can contact the show by emailing correspondence@kermodeandmayo.com or you can find us on social media, @KermodeandMayo.
EXCLUSIVE NordVPN Deal ➼ https://nordvpn.com/take Try it risk-free now with a 30-day money-back guarantee!
A Sony Music Entertainment production.
Find more great podcasts from Sony Music Entertainment at sonymusic.com/podcasts and follow us @sonypodcasts
To advertise on this show contact: podcastadsales@sonymusic.com

00:00:00 Introduction 00:09:04 Pinocchio Unstrung review 00:15:49 Box Office Top Ten 00:31:00 Andy Serkis interview 00:48:20 Animal Farm review 01:02:58 Laughter Lift 01:09:01 Ask E. Jean review

item.title: Christopher Nolan's Odyssey Is Inconsequential
item.author: The House of Tabula
item.content:encoded:
Christopher Nolan's Odyssey is a technologically astonishing, fundamentally flawed, emotionally shallow grand spectacle. As such, it is the definitive film of modern cinema.
Support The House of Tabula (and join our Discord): https://www.patreon.com/c/thehouseoftabula
Join this channel to get access to perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCL5kBJmBUVFLYBDiSiK1VDw/join
Lewis' streams: https://www.twitch.tv/lewismichaelbond
For business inquiries: contact@thehouseoftabula.com

item.title: JOHN SCHWARTZMAN - Cinematographer
item.content:encoded: SEASON 2 - EPISODE 203 John Schwartzman - CinematographerIn this episode of the Team Deakins Podcast, we speak with cinematographer John Schwartzman (THE HOUSEMAID, THE HIGHWAYMEN, THE ROCK). John has worked on numerous high-budget, highly commercial films throughout his career, and we spend much of the episode discussing his approach to his work on those movies and his process overall. John's father, an entertainment lawyer for the likes of Stanley Kubrick and Hal Ashby, married into the Coppola filmmaking family while John was just a teenager, and we learn how John leveraged this connection and his skills at board games to win letters of recommendation from his uncle Francis Ford Coppola and friend-of-the-family George Lucas. We also discuss how John draws on his upbringing to help actors feel comfortable during a shoot, and he reflects on how he satisfies his creative needs in modern Hollywood. John also shares the pivotal pieces of advice he received from cinematographers such as Conrad Hall and Caleb Deschanel (Season 1, Episode 86) that instilled confidence in him, and we learn how and why he failed out of USC.

item.title: Is The Odyssey Any Good?
item.author: The Kino Corner
item.content:encoded:

item.title: Mindy Kaling’s Closet Picks
item.author: CRITERION
item.content:encoded:
Subscribe to Criterion: https://bit.ly/4vlR0aj
The actor, writer, and producer talks about being blown away by Sandra Hüller’s performance in ANATOMY OF A FALL, shares her attraction to the romance in LOVE & BASKETBALL, and praises MISSISSIPPI MASALA as being ahead of its time.
Mindy’s Picks: ANATOMY OF A FALL (Justine Triet, 2023) LOVE & BASKETBALL (Gina Prince-Bythewood, 2000) FRANCES HA (Noah Baumbach, 2013) A ROOM WITH A VIEW (James Ivory, 1986) THE SHAPE OF WATER (Guillermo del Toro, 2017) MISSISSIPPI MASALA (Mira Nair, 1991) PICKUP ON SOUTH STREET (Sam Fuller, 1953)
All are available from criterion.com: https://www.criterion.com/shop/collection/983-mindy-kaling-s-closet-picks
And FRANCES HA, A ROOM WITH A VIEW, and MISSISSIPPI MASALA are currently playing on our streaming service, The Criterion Channel: https://www.criterionchannel.com

item.title: Half in the Bag: The Odyssey
item.author: RedLetterMedia
item.content:encoded: Christopher Nolan made a new movie. It is called The Odyssey. It was shot on IMAX. Did you know that? Did you know any of that?

item.title: Camila Mendes’s Closet Picks
item.author: CRITERION
item.content:encoded:
Subscribe to Criterion: https://bit.ly/4vlR0aj
The actor praises Nicole Kidman’s spellbinding performance in THE OTHERS, finds beauty and inspiration in Lila Avilés’s TÓTEM, and shares how THE BEFORE TRILOGY perfectly captures the nuances of falling in love.
Camila’s Picks: THE GREAT BEAUTY (Paolo Sorrentino, 2013) TRIANGLE OF SADNESS (Ruben Östlund, 2022) THE OTHERS (Alejandro Amenábar, 2001) TÓTEM (Lila Avilés, 2023) THE BEFORE TRILOGY (Richard Linklater)
All are available from criterion.com, with THE GREAT BEAUTY and TÓTEM currently playing on our streaming service, The Criterion Channel: https://www.criterionchannel.com

item.title: Mark Kermode reviews Synthetic Sincerity
item.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
item.content:encoded:
#MarkKermode #SimonMayo #FilmReviews
In a highly entertaining and profoundly moving film, Marc Isaacs (The Filmmaker's House) makes a deal with The Synthetic Sincerity Lab, an AI research project affiliated with the University of Southern England, where researchers are investigating the possibility of teaching AI characters authenticity, using characters from Isaacs' documentaries to do so
To hear more, follow Kermode and Mayo's Take wherever you get your podcasts: https://podfollow.com/1616559297

item.title: When an Actor Walked Away From Batman – Michael Keaton
item.author: FilmStack
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Storyblocks for sponsoring this video. To get 15% off any annual plan for a limited time, go to: http://storyblocks.com/FilmStack
Michael Keaton became a movie star by taking a role almost nobody wanted him to play.
By the late 80s, he was known for comedies like Night Shift, Mr. Mom, and Beetlejuice. So when he was cast as Batman, fans hated it. They thought Hollywood had turned the Dark Knight into a joke before cameras even started rolling.
Then the movie came out. Keaton proved everyone wrong, became part of one of the biggest films of the decade, and had the kind of role most actors spend their whole career chasing. But instead of holding onto it, he walked away.
And that choice changed everything. For years, Keaton kept working, but Hollywood slowly lost a clear idea of where he fit. Then decades later, the role that brought him back used the one thing he couldn’t fully escape.
This is the story of when an actor had to prove himself twice. This is Michael Keaton.
Chapters: 00:00 - Intro 00:47 - I’m Batman 07:39 - Walking Away 13:04 - I'm Birdman
Michael Keaton. Batman 1989. Tim Burton. Best Actor Oscar Nomination. Beetlejuice. Mr Mom. Multiplicity. Spotlight. Beetlejuice Beetlejuice. The Founder. Spiderman Villain. Homecoming. Vulture. Birdman. Jackie Brown. The Other Guys. Dopesick. Emmy Winning Actor.
If you like this video don't forget to leave a like, and if you're interested in videos about movies and the film industry in general, make sure to subscribe to FilmStack for more great content.
If you have any other ideas for videos, leave a comment and I might make a video with your idea.
Patreon: https://patreon.com/FilmStack Twitter: https://twitter.com/realFilmStack Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/realfilmstack/
#michaelkeaton #actor #oscars

item.title: Is THE ODYSSEY truly epic? With SIR CHRISTOPHER NOLAN
item.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
item.content:encoded:
The Take is now on Patreon: www.patreon.com/kermodeandmayo Become a Vanguardista or an Ultra Vanguardista to get video episodes of Take Two every week, plus member-only chat rooms, polls and submissions to influence the show, behind-the-scenes photos and videos, the monthly Redactor’s Roundup newsletter, and access to a new fortnightly LIVE show - a raucous, unfiltered lunchtime special with the Good Doctors, new features, and live chat so you can heckle, vote, and have your questions read out in real time.
Ben Bailey Smith sits in for Simon Mayo this week.
The time is upon us for the biggest and most epic cinematic event of the year: Sir Christopher Nolan’s Odyssey is finally hitting the (very) big screen—shot on IMAX film, no less. With Matt Damon as Odysseus, Anne Hathaway as Penelope, and a smattering of other screen superstars including Tom Holland, Robert Pattinson, Lupita Nyong’o, and Zendaya in the cast, it’s the cinematic event we’ve all been waiting for. Not to mention Samantha Morton who, as you’ll hear, might just steal the show… Strap in for a bumper review from Mark and Ben—plus of course Simon’s interview with director Sir Christopher Nolan himself. He tells us all about his decades long journey with this ancient story, bringing myth to life, and wrestling with the gods in this massive movie.
Synthetic Sincerity is our second film up for review—a docufiction curio about a filmmaker’s deal with an AI company that hovers somewhere between fiction and reality. Plus, Mark and Ben will run down the box office top 10, witter around your excellent correspondence, and subject us all to the Laughter Lift again—although we can blame/credit (delete as applicable) you guys for some of the jokes this time. Thanks as always for being part of the Witterverse!
You can contact the show by emailing correspondence@kermodeandmayo.com or you can find us on social media, @KermodeandMayo.
EXCLUSIVE NordVPN Deal ➼ https://nordvpn.com/take Try it risk-free now with a 30-day money-back guarantee!

Here’s the link for tickets: https://www.fane.co.uk/kermode-and-mayo
Timecodes: 00:06:52 Synthetic Sincerity review 00:22:51 Box Office 10 00:40:02 Sir Christopher Nolan interview 00:56:09 The Odyssey review 01:19:19 Laughter Lift--

item.title: Devonté Hynes (Blood Orange) & Thomas Bangalter (Daft Punk) | The A24 Podcast
item.author: A24
item.content:encoded:
SUBSCRIBE: http://bit.ly/A24subscribe
Topics covered include: Devonté’s cello score as the fifth lead in The Invite, Daft Punk working with an 85 piece orchestra on Tron: Legacy (also starring Olivia Wilde!), finding the magic chemistry between strings and dialogue, monophonic vs. polyphonic, a great note from a Disney executive, extreme precision, the true effort it takes to bring soul to synthesizers, composing the Mythologies ballet score before the choreography existed, Random Access Memories being made like a film without cameras, the 250 tracks on Daft Punk’s “Touch,” spending two years with just a piano and GoPro, restoring Electroma in 35mm for Tribeca Film Festival, and curiosity as an antidote to the algorithm.
A film written, directed by and starring Olivia Wilde with Seth Rogen, Penélope Cruz and Edward Norton. THE INVITE – Now playing in theaters.
GET TICKETS: theinvite.movie ------
ABOUT A24: The studio behind EVERYTHING EVERYWHERE ALL AT ONCE, UNCUT GEMS, MIDSOMMAR, MOONLIGHT, BEEF on Netflix, EUPHORIA on Max & more.
Now Playing: BACKROOMS, THE DEATH OF ROBIN HOOD, THE INVITE Coming Soon: TONY, PRIMETIME, ONSLAUGHT, THE DEBUT & MORE
Subscribe to A24's NEWSLETTER: http://bit.ly/A24signup Sign up for A24 MEMBERSHIP: aaa24.com Visit A24 WEBSITE: http://bit.ly/A24filmsdotcom Like A24 on FACEBOOK: http://bit.ly/FBA24 Follow A24 on TWITTER: http://bit.ly/TweetA24 Follow A24 on INSTAGRAM: http://bit.ly/InstaA24

item.title: CHRISTINA ALEXANDRA VOROS - Director / Cinematographer
item.content:encoded: SEASON 2 - EPISODE 202 Christina Alexandra Voros - Director / CinematographerIn this episode of the Team Deakins Podcast, we speak with director and cinematographer Christina Alexandra Voros (DUTTON RANCH, THE MADISON, YELLOWSTONE). A graduate of the NYU film program (Season 1, Episode 104), Christina discovered cinematography and directing while at school, and she walks us through her formative years there. After working her way through documentaries and features, Christina found steady work directing and shooting shows within the Taylor Sheridan universe, and we discuss the unique working conditions of those series and how she approaches doing two jobs at once. She reflects on what she brings to narrative films and series from her time in documentaries, and we discuss the flexibility one has when you're both the director and the cinematographer. We later learn up to five cameras can be rolling at once on a show like THE MADISON and DUTTON RANCH, and Christina helps us understand how you can direct a scene with so much simultaneous coverage.

item.title: John Leguizamo’s Closet Picks
item.author: CRITERION
item.content:encoded: The actor, writer, and director shares what makes Michael Haneke and Robert Bresson such exceptional filmmakers, praises the cinematography of Alfonso Cuarón’s ROMA, and talks about the power of representation in DO THE RIGHT THING and EL NORTE.

item.title: Hollywood's Next Gold Rush: Spooky Internet Memes
item.author: RedLetterMedia
item.content:encoded: Sometimes we have a lot to say about a specific topic, and sometimes we just sit Rich Evans down in front of a camera without him having any idea what we're gong to talk about and then force him to not be creative at all. In this Black Void episode, Mike, Rich and Jay discuss how, in the wake of Backrooms' success, movie studios are already hard at work trying to scoop up as many popular spooky internet things that they can.

item.title: Finneas’s Closet Picks
item.author: CRITERION
item.content:encoded: The musician and composer reminisces about watching A HARD DAY'S NIGHT with his dad, explores the beautifully unnerving score for EYES WIDE SHUT, and shares what he learned from collaborating with Alfonso Cuarón.

item.title: I Watched MAGA's Favorite Movie
item.author: Like Stories of Old
item.content:encoded:
Watch 16 minutes of extended thoughts on Citizen Vigilante exclusively on Nebula: https://nebula.tv/videos/lsoo-extended-thoughts-on-citizen-vigilante?ref=lsoo Get 50% off a full year of Nebula at https://go.nebula.tv/lsoo
0:00 I watched Citizen Vigilante 2:25 This is not a well-made movie 5:25 Constructing an echo chamber 7:59 It's just not what it pretends to be 11:17 The vigilante is... weird 15:16 A bottomless pit of resentment 18:44 They don't actually care about victims 21:12 The tragedy of unending self-humiliation
Watch this video ad-free on Nebula: https://nebula.tv/videos/lsoo-i-watched-magas-favorite-movie
-- SECOND CHANNEL -- @LikeStoriesUntold

item.title: Are we in for a MOAN-A from Mark about live action remakes?
item.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
item.content:encoded:
The Take is now on Patreon: www.patreon.com/kermodeandmayo Become a Vanguardista or an Ultra Vanguardista to get video episodes of Take Two every week, plus member-only chat rooms, polls and submissions to influence the show, behind-the-scenes photos and videos, the monthly Redactor’s Roundup newsletter, and access to a new fortnightly LIVE show—a raucous, unfiltered lunchtime special with the Good Doctors, new features, and live chat so you can heckle, vote, and have your questions read out in real time.
Disney animated smash Moana is getting the live action treatment this week—and our guest is director Thomas Kail, who previously helmed both the screen and stage versions of Lin Manuel Miranda’s Hamilton and In The Heights too. He talks to Simon about their longstanding collaboration, the whole island of creatives it took to make this movie, and discovering the perfect Moana from thousands of auditionees in Catherine Laga’aia. Plus Mark reviews it—will he be charmed, or is this one live action remake too far?
Also on the review slate this week we’ve got Rosebush Pruning, a twisted indie family drama featuring all the bodily fluids, and Evil Dead Burn—the latest in the super-influential zombie series. Is the franchise unkillable, or a dead man walking? Don’t miss Mark’s verdict.
All that plus the box office top 10, the weekly trial of the Laughter Lift (sorry), and of course plenty of correspondence from you lot. Keep it coming!
You can contact the show by emailing correspondence@kermodeandmayo.com or you can find us on social media, @KermodeandMayo.

Timecodes: 00:00:00 Introduction 00:10:46 Rosebush Pruning 00:19:41 Box Office Top Ten 00:36:16 Thomas Kail interview 00:51:47 Moana review 01:01:36 Laughter Lift 01:04:10 Evil Dead Burn

item.title: DEAN GOODINE - Prop Master
item.content:encoded: SEASON 2 - EPISODE 201 Dean Goodine - Prop MasterIn this episode of the Team Deakins Podcast, we speak with prop master Dean Goodine (THE ORDER, THE ASSASSINATION OF JESSE JAMES, UNFORGIVEN). From a small lumber town in Canada, Dean almost committed his life to the oil fields of Alberta before turning to film. Throughout the episode, Dean helps us understand what a prop master does for a movie, and, drawing on his extensive experience with firearms safety, we also discuss how he keeps sets safe. While learning what Dean's responsibilities are as a prop master, he shares how he collaborates with the costume department, and we also discuss how he adapts to working with different directors. In addition to film, Dean has also worked in television, and he reflects on dealing with the phenomenon of fandom while working on STARGATE. We also discuss the state of the Canadian film industry, historical and present-day, and Dean reveals how his own research and prep process has stayed the same. We worked with Dean on JESSE JAMES, and we had a great time catching up with him.If you enjoyed listening to Dean, you can learn more about him and his career in his book "They Don't Pay Me To Say No: My Life in Film and Television Props"

item.title: David Tennant’s Closet Picks
item.author: CRITERION
item.content:encoded: The actor praises Terry Gilliam’s extraordinary imagination, talks about the subtle beauty and intelligence of BRIEF ENCOUNTER, and examines what made Laurence Olivier’s Shakespearean performances so remarkable.

item.title: Best of the Worst: Plinketto 13
item.author: RedLetterMedia
item.content:encoded: Talk about dropping the ball!!! The boys are back to pick three feature films to watch and yikes....Was it a night of uncomfortablenessocity. I mean, the first film was fine, but the last two. Oh my god. Especially the second movie. What kind of weird people are out there!?!? What makes people make movies like this!? Movies I can't unsee or forget. My brain is poisoned forever. So will yours be as well. Thank YouTube we had to censor most of this filthy, embarrassing garbage. For f*cks sake!
